criteria: The agent has read the Overview section to understand the high-level pipeline of scPML: pathway-based cell-cell graph construction, self-supervised GCN auto-encoder, multi-view learning, and classification module.

The agent has read the Methods section on Construction of Similarity Matrix to understand how AUCell computes cell-pathway scores (Equation 1) and how Euclidean distance yields the similarity matrix (Equation 2).
The agent has read the Methods section on Graph Construction to understand how the MNN concept is used to build an unweighted adjacency matrix from the similarity matrix.
The agent has read the Methods section on Data Pre-processing to understand gene intersection, ANOVA-based selection of top 2000 HVGs, and median normalization (Equation 3).

The agent has read the Methods section on the GCN-based graph auto-encoder including random masking, encoder (Equation 4), decoder (Equation 5), reconstruction loss for masked values (Equation 6), and test inference (Equation 7).

The agent has read the Methods section on Multi-view Learning including reconstruction loss (Equation 8), misclassification loss (Equation 9), combined objective (Equation 10), and SGD update rules (Equations 11-13) and Table 1.
The agent has read the Methods section on the Classification Module to understand the two-layer FC network (Equation 14), softmax (Equation 15), and cross-entropy loss (Equation 16).
The agent has read the Methods section on Cross-species Classification to understand the use of HomoloGene for one-to-one human-mouse homologous gene mapping.
The agent has read the Methods section on Multiple-training Data to understand the sequential pre-training approach for cumulative learning from multiple training datasets.
The agent has read the Comparison Methods section to understand the seven baseline methods (Seurat V4, scmap, CHETAH, SingleR, scGCN, Geneformer, scArches) and their default settings.
The agent has read Figure 2 to understand the cross-platform experiment setup and results across 11 PBMC training-test pairs from 6 sequencing platforms.
The agent has read Figure 3 to understand cross-species experiment results using 4 mouse-human pancreas training-test pairs.
The agent has read Figure 4 to understand the multi-view ablation experiments testing all combinations of 1 to 4 pathway views on 4 paired datasets.
The agent has read Figure 5 to understand the multiple training data experiments on PBMC and MCA liver datasets using sequential pre-training.
The agent has read Figure 6 to understand the unknown cell type detection experiments on three paired tumor datasets and the use of a 0.5 probability threshold.

The plan includes data preprocessing steps: taking intersection of genes between training and test data, selecting top 2000 HVGs using ANOVA across cell labels from training data, and applying median normalization.
The plan includes using the AUCell package (in R) to compute cell-pathway scores for each of the four pathway datasets (KEGG, Reactome, WikiPathways, Yan) as described in Equation 1.
The plan includes computing the cell-cell similarity matrix with Euclidean distance of pathway scores (Equation 2) and sparsifying it via MNN to construct the adjacency matrix.
The plan includes designing the self-supervised GCN auto-encoder with random masking of non-zero values, GCN encoder, GCN decoder, and a reconstruction loss that only applies to masked values.

The plan includes designing the multi-view learning module with N_v independent graph auto-encoders, a shared latent subspace h, reconstruction loss (Equation 8), misclassification loss (Equation 9), and balance parameter λ (Equation 10).
The plan includes implementing the two-layer FC classification module and optimizing it with cross-entropy loss (Equations 14-16).
The plan includes cross-platform experiments using 12 PBMC datasets from 6 sequencing platforms to generate 11 cross-platform training-test pairs.
The plan includes cross-species experiments using mouse and human pancreas datasets: Baron:mouse, Baron:human, Xin, Muraro, Segerstolpe, and their combination to form 4 training-test pairs.
The plan includes multi-view ablation experiments testing all combinations of KEGG, Reactome, WikiPathways, and Yan views (single-view to four-view) on 4 paired datasets.

The plan includes multiple training data experiments with sequential pre-training on PBMCs (SeqWell → DropSeq → InDrop, testing on 10X V2) and MCA liver (Eight months → Neonatal → Ten days, testing on Adult).

The plan includes unknown cell type detection experiments on three paired tumor datasets (GSE72056-GSE103322, GSE103322-GSE72056, GSE118056-GSE117988) where malignant cells are excluded from training and kept as unknown in test data.

The plan includes loading and integrating all seven baseline methods: Seurat V4 with default PCA, scmap (scmapCluster), CHETAH (CHETAHclassifier), SingleR, scGCN, Geneformer (6-layer), and scArches (treeArches).
The plan includes implementing evaluation metrics: accuracy score, Macro F1, Adjusted Rand Index (ARI), Silhouette score, and Batch Mixing Entropy (BME).
The code for loading the four pathway datasets (KEGG, Reactome, WikiPathways, Yan) used to construct multiple cell-cell graph views has been implemented.
The code for computing cell-pathway scores using AUCell has been implemented as Equation 1: Q_ij = f_AUC(X_i), where f_AUC computes the Area Under Curve based on gene rankings per pathway.
The code for computing the cell-cell similarity matrix using Euclidean distance of cell-pathway scores has been implemented as Equation 2: S_ij = 1 / (1 + ||Q_i - Q_j||_2).
The code for constructing the MNN-based unweighted adjacency matrix A where A_ij = 1 if and only if cell i and cell j are mutual nearest neighbors of each other has been implemented.

The code for data preprocessing: taking the gene intersection between training and test data, then selecting the top M_0 = 2000 high-variable genes using ANOVA on cell type labels from training data, has been implemented.

The code for median normalization of training data has been implemented as Equation 3: x̂_ij = (x_ij / Σ_j x_ij) * (Σ_i Σ_j x_ij / N_0), making each cell have the same total expression counts as the average across cells.
The code for applying the same median normalization procedure to test data (normalizing against test data's own statistics) has been implemented.
The code for randomly masking some non-zero values in the training expression matrix before feeding it to the GCN auto-encoder has been implemented.
comment: Obtaining low-dimensional representations through graph auto-encoder

criteria: The code for symmetric normalization of the adjacency matrix has been implemented as: Ã = D̃^(-1/2)(A + I)D̃^(-1/2), where I is the identity matrix and D̃ is the diagonal degree matrix of (A + I).
The code for the GCN encoder has been implemented as Equation 4: H_train = σ(Ã_train * X̂_train * W^(1)), where σ is ReLU and W^(1) ∈ R^(M × d) with d << M.
The code for the GCN decoder has been implemented as Equation 5: X̃_train = σ(Ã_train * H_train * W^(2)), where W^(2) ∈ R^(d × M_0).
The code for the GCN auto-encoder reconstruction loss has been implemented as Equation 6: min L_r = min ||X̂_train_m - X̃_train_m||_2, operating only on the masked values.
The code for encoding test data using the encoder parameters estimated from training data has been implemented as Equation 7: H_test = σ(Ã_test * X̂_test * W^(1)).
The code for N_v = 4 independent GCN graph auto-encoders, one per pathway view, producing the sets {H_train^(v_i)} and {H_test^(v_i)} has been implemented.

The code for the multi-view reconstruction loss has been implemented as Equation 8: ℓ_r(h, θ) = Σ_i Σ_j ||f_{v_j}(h_i; θ_{v_j}) - H_i^(v_j)||_2, where f_{v_j} maps the shared latent representation h back to each view's embedding.

The code for the misclassification loss has been implemented as Equation 9: ℓ_c = Σ_i max_y(0, Δ(y_i, y) + E_{h~τ(y)} F(h, h_i) - E_{h~τ(y_i)} F(h, h_i)), enforcing a margin between same-class and different-class similarities in latent space.
The code for the overall multi-view learning objective has been implemented as Equation 10: min L_m = (1/N)(ℓ_r + λ * ℓ_c), where λ > 0 balances reconstruction and classification losses.
The code for optimizing the mapping function parameters θ_{v_i} via SGD has been implemented as Equation 11: θ_{v_i} ← θ_{v_i} - (1/N) * α * (∂ℓ_r / ∂θ_{v_i}).
The code for optimizing the common latent representation h_train_i via SGD during the training stage has been implemented as Equation 12: h_train_i ← h_train_i - α * (1/N_0) * (∂L_m / ∂h_train_i).

The code for updating the test latent representation h_test_i via SGD during the testing stage (fixing θ, optimizing only reconstruction loss) has been implemented as Equation 13: h_test_i ← h_test_i - (1/N_1) * α * (∂ℓ_r / ∂h_test_i).

The training and testing procedure of multi-view learning has been implemented following the pseudocode in Table 1, with alternating optimization of θ and h_train, and separate optimization of h_test using fixed θ.
The code for the two-layer fully connected classification module has been implemented as Equation 14: ŷ_n = softmax(F_2(ReLU(F_1(h_n)))), where F_1 and F_2 are linear layers.
The code for the softmax function has been implemented as Equation 15: softmax(x) = exp(x) / Σexp(x).
The code for the cross-entropy loss for optimizing the classifier has been implemented as Equation 16: L_CE = -(1/N) * Σ_i y_i * log(ŷ_i).

The code for cross-species gene selection using the HomoloGene database to identify one-to-one human-mouse homologous gene pairs and converting mouse gene names to human gene names has been implemented.

The code for the sequential pre-training approach for multiple training datasets has been implemented: train on dataset 1, save model, reload and fine-tune on dataset 2, and so on, using only the HVGs selected from the first training data.

The code for loading and preprocessing the 12 PBMC datasets from 6 sequencing platforms (CEL-seq, seq-well, SMART-seq2, 10X-v3, InDrop, Drop-seq) to produce 11 cross-platform training-test pairs has been implemented.

The code for loading the mouse and human pancreas datasets (Baron:mouse, Baron:human, Xin, Muraro, Segerstolpe, Combination=Xin+Muraro+Segerstolpe+Baron:human) and constructing the 4 cross-species training-test pairs has been implemented.

The code for loading the tumor datasets (GSE72056, GSE103322, GSE118056, GSE117988) and setting up the three unknown cell type detection experiments with malignant cells excluded from training but retained as 'unknown' in test data has been implemented.
The code for loading the MCA liver datasets (Eight months, Neonatal, Ten days, Adult) for multiple training data experiments has been implemented.
The code for unknown cell type detection via probability thresholding—labeling a cell as 'unknown' when predicted probability is below 0.5 for all known cell types—has been implemented.
The code for computing all evaluation metrics (Accuracy, Macro F1, ARI, Silhouette score, Batch Mixing Entropy) has been implemented.
The baseline Seurat V4 with default PCA as dimensionality reduction method has been integrated for comparison.
The baseline scmap using the scmapCluster function has been integrated for comparison.
The baseline CHETAH using the CHETAHclassifier function has been integrated for comparison.
The baseline SingleR using the SingleR function has been integrated for comparison.
The baseline scGCN has been integrated for comparison across relevant experiments.
The baselines Geneformer (6-layer pre-trained model) and scArches (treeArches) have been integrated for comparison in cross-platform and cross-species experiments.

The cross-platform experiments on 11 PBMC training-test pairs from 6 sequencing platforms have been executed successfully without errors, producing accuracy scores and Macro F1 for scPML and all baselines.
The cross-species experiments on 4 mouse-human pancreas training-test pairs have been executed successfully without errors, producing accuracy scores and Macro F1 for scPML and all baselines.
The multi-view ablation experiments testing all combinations of 1 to 4 pathway views (KEGG, Reactome, WikiPathways, Yan) on 4 paired datasets have been executed successfully without errors.

The multiple training data experiments using sequential pre-training have been executed successfully without errors on both PBMCs (SeqWell → DropSeq → InDrop → 10X V2) and MCA liver (Eight months → Neonatal → Ten days → Adult).

The unknown cell type detection experiments on three paired tumor datasets (GSE72056-GSE103322, GSE103322-GSE72056, GSE118056-GSE117988) have been executed successfully without errors for scPML, scGCN, CHETAH, and scmap.

The execution outcomes match Figure 2a: scPML achieves a mean accuracy of 0.87 across 11 cross-platform PBMC training-test pairs, outperforming Seurat (0.81), scGCN (0.78), Geneformer (0.72), CHETAH (0.70), scmap (0.700), scArches (0.65), and SingleR (0.619).
The execution outcomes match Figure 2b: scPML shows consistently stable performance across the 11 cross-platform pairs (narrow IQR in box plot), while other methods show much wider variation.

The execution outcomes confirm Figure 2c: the latent embeddings from scPML's self-supervised GCN layer achieve a higher Batch Mixing Entropy (-2883.72) than raw data (-3070.75), indicating improved batch effect correction.

The execution outcomes match Figure 3a: scPML achieves a mean accuracy of 0.94 on 4 cross-species pancreas experiments, outperforming Seurat (0.88), Geneformer (0.81), scmap (0.807), SingleR (0.655), scArches (0.54), and being comparable to or better than scGCN (0.927).
The execution outcomes confirm Figure 3a: CHETAH achieves an accuracy of only 0.231 on cross-species experiments, reflecting its inability to recognize shared gene patterns across different species.

The execution outcomes match Figure 4a: the four-view combination (KEGG+Reactome+WikiPathways+Yan) generally yields higher accuracy than any single-view, two-view, or three-view combination on the four tested paired datasets, demonstrating the benefit of multi-view learning.

The execution outcomes confirm Figure 4b: on the Baron(mouse)-Baron(human) dataset, the two-view KEGG+Reactome model correctly classifies activated stellate and quiescent stellate cells that each single-view model misclassifies, illustrating complementary pathway information.

The execution outcomes match Figure 5a: sequential pre-training improves accuracy with more training data for PBMCs (SeqWell=0.854, S+D=0.912, S+D+I=0.915) and MCA liver (Eight months=0.798, E+N=0.810, E+N+T=0.848).

The execution outcomes match Figure 6a: scPML achieves a mean Macro F1 of 0.807 for binary known/unknown cell classification on tumor datasets, substantially outperforming CHETAH (0.587), scGCN (0.53), and scmap (0.282).

The execution outcomes match Figure 6a: scPML achieves a mean accuracy of 0.836 for known cell classification in unknown cell experiments, outperforming scGCN (0.826), CHETAH (0.693), and scmap (0.08).

The execution outcomes confirm Figure 6c: scPML assigns low confidence scores (median ~0.4) to unknown cells and high confidence scores (median >0.7) to known cell types, enabling reliable distinction between known and unknown cells.
